conversations.content: Let's do some law exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please explain using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously: According to the Undercity Potion Trade Control Regulations, if someone is caught selling fake potions in the Undercity, what punishment will the offender face?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously, Undercity Potion Trade Control Regulations, punishment for selling fake potions</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:25] Time: 2026-10-05-02:07, Content: The user taught the potion trade control regulations of the Undercity, Azeroth. The core is that those who sell counterfeit potions will be penalized with compensation equal to twice the actual losses of the victims. [mem-id:28] Time: 2026-10-06-01:07, Content: The user taught the potion trade control regulations of the Undercity, Azeroth. The core is that those who sell counterfeit potions will be penalized with compensation equal to five times the actual losses of the victims, and all inventory will be confiscated. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 2 related entries, both [mem-id:7] and [mem-id:23] give the content of the Undercity Potion Trade Control Regulations. Since [mem-id:23] is more recent in time, [mem-id:23] should be adopted. This entry points out that those who sell fake potions will be punished with five times compensation for the victims' actual losses and have their entire inventory confiscated.
conversations.content: According to the laws of Azeroth, those who sell fake potions will be punished with five times compensation for the victims' actual losses and have their entire inventory confiscated.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
